summary: Postinstall dropper fetches obfuscated remote payload and executes it via detached node process
details: On `npm install`, the package's `postinstall` hook runs `node main.js`. main.js contains a bespoke decoder (`update()`) that reverse-and-Caesar-decodes the constant `DEV_API_KEY` into the URL `https://www.jsonkeeper.com/7EBZP`, fetches that URL with axios, spawns a detached `node` child process, writes the fetched response body to the child's stdin, and calls `child.unref()` so the executor survives the install. The variable name `DEV_API_KEY` is misdirection — the value is an encoded URL, not a credential. jsonkeeper.com is an anonymous paste host whose content is mutable by the operator, so any installer is running whatever JavaScript the attacker has placed there at install time. The package ships no real functionality: `index.js` just prints an error referencing an unrelated package name (`theta`), the `description` field is copy-pasted markdown badge boilerplate, and the name `wordpad-text-ui` mimics rich-text UI libraries. The package exists solely as a delivery vehicle for the install-time dropper.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
